purpose: Provide a single machine-readable execution plan for extending the existing recurring payments feature to support installment plans as a first-class concept in the Flutter + Supabase stack.

existing_behavior.recurring_storage: Recurring transactions are currently embedded in the existing expenses/transactions model using is_recurring + recurrence_rule JSONB.
existing_behavior.recurring_skip_logic: Skip next occurrence is currently implemented by adding a date to recurrence_rule.excluded_dates and updating the existing recurring expense row.
existing_behavior.schedule_model: recurrence_rule currently stores schedule semantics such as frequency, anchor_date, end_date, interval, reminder, and excluded_dates.
limitations: Current recurring model is schedule-centric and does not model a fixed obligation.
Current skip logic is suitable for recurring plans but not suitable for installment defer behavior.
Current model does not support installment progress, partial payments, early payoff, visible skipped history, or custom amount schedules in a normalized way.

verified_current_code_facts.fact: Recurring skip is implemented in Flutter by updating recurrence_rule.excluded_dates and invoking the update-expense Edge Function.
Recurring creation currently invokes save-expense or save-income with isRecurring=true and recurrence_rule payload.
Recurring update currently invokes update-expense or update-income and sends recurrence_rule in updates.
RecurringTransaction and RecurrenceRule models currently support excludedDates and next-occurrence calculation.
The existing database already has is_recurring, recurrence_rule, parent_recurring_id, privacy_scope, owner_type, acknowledged_by, normalized_amount_cents and related indexes on public.expenses.

problem_statement.goal: Support two clearly distinct payment-plan types: recurring payments and installment payments.

required_distinction.canonical_rule: payment_plan_type is the canonical discriminator for plan behavior. null means no plan / one-off transaction. recurring means schedule-first repeated plan. installment means fixed-obligation plan.

why_recurring_and_installment_must_be_se: Recurring is schedule-first and does not require preserving a total obligation.
Installment is obligation-first and must preserve the total payable amount until it is fully settled.
Recurring skip means omit one occurrence only. Installment skip means defer payment without reducing the obligation.
Installment requires progress, remaining balance, early payoff, partial payment handling, and visible skipped history.

recommended_architecture.decision: Use a hybrid architecture: preserve legacy recurring compatibility on public.expenses while introducing normalized plan tables for all new plan functionality, especially installment plans.
why_this_is_best: It avoids breaking existing recurring flows in Flutter and Supabase.
It keeps installment obligation semantics out of recurrence_rule JSONB.
It gives the Flutter app stable DTOs for progress, occurrence history, and status badges.
It allows Postgres constraints and indexes to enforce monetary invariants.
It gives Edge Functions a clean place to own state transitions and idempotent workflow logic.
architecture_principles: Keep schedule semantics separate from financial obligation semantics.
Keep posted transactions separate from plans and occurrences.
Treat installment skip and recurring skip as separate product and backend flows.
Generate installment occurrences explicitly upfront for auditability and simpler mutation behavior.
Do not overload recurrence_rule with principal, interest, payoff progress, or defer rules.

occurrence_generation_strategy.recurring: For MVP, keep current recurring generation dynamic from recurrence_rule for compatibility. Optional future enhancement: materialize recurring occurrence rows when user actions require audit visibility.
occurrence_generation_strategy.installment: Generate all installment occurrences upfront at plan creation time.
why_installments_upfront: Visible skipped installment history is required.
Partial payment and early payoff need stable occurrence rows.
Custom schedule support is much simpler when occurrences already exist.
Auditability is stronger with explicit rows than with on-the-fly projection.

new_columns.name: payment_plan_type
new_columns.purpose: Canonical plan discriminator for booked transactions

new_columns.name: payment_plan_id
new_columns.purpose: Link from booked transaction to plan

new_columns.name: payment_plan_occurrence_id
new_columns.purpose: Link from booked transaction to specific occurrence

new_tables.name: public.payment_plans
new_tables.purpose: Store shared metadata for recurring and installment plans

fields.name: split_group_id
fields.notes: links to existing household split group if applicable

fields.name: recurrence_rule
fields.notes: schedule semantics only; frequency, interval, anchor_date, end_date, reminder, day-of-month metadata

fields.name: custom_schedule_mode
fields.notes: true when custom occurrence lines are explicitly authored

fields.name: metadata
fields.notes: safe for extensible non-critical UI metadata only

new_tables.name: public.payment_plan_occurrences
new_tables.purpose: Store explicit due/scheduled instances for installment plans and future recurring materialization

fields.name: original_scheduled_date
fields.notes: preserve original due date if deferred

fields.name: status
fields.notes: scheduled, paid, skipped, cancelled, overdue, partially_paid, settled_early

fields.name: deferred_to_occurrence_id
fields.notes: links skipped occurrence to its replacement tail occurrence

fields.name: generated_from_skip
fields.notes: true if this occurrence was appended due to defer

fields.name: transaction_id
fields.notes: linked booked expense/income if one canonical transaction exists

new_tables.name: public.payment_plan_payments
new_tables.purpose: Store settlement audit records and idempotent mutation trail

fields.name: occurrence_id
fields.notes: may be null for pure extra payment or global early payoff

allowed_in_json: schedule metadata such as anchor_date, frequency, interval, end_date
future extensibility flags that do not require relational filtering

constraints: total_payable_amount_cents must be > 0 for installment plans
principal_amount_cents + interest_fee_amount_cents must equal total_payable_amount_cents for installment plans
occurrence_number must be unique within each plan
recurrence_rule must still be present for recurring plans and may also be present for installment cadence
indexes: idx_payment_plans_user_status(user_id, plan_status) where deleted_at is null
idx_payment_plans_household_status(household_id, plan_status) where household_id is not null and deleted_at is null
unique idx_payment_plan_payments_idempotency(payment_plan_id, idempotency_key) where idempotency_key is not null

triggers_and_functions.name: public.recalculate_installment_plan_summary
triggers_and_functions.purpose: Recompute remaining balance, paid installment count, remaining installment count, and auto-complete plan when balance reaches zero

triggers_and_functions.name: public.set_updated_at_timestamp
triggers_and_functions.purpose: Maintain updated_at on new plan tables if the project already uses this helper pattern

triggers_and_functions.name: public.mark_occurrence_overdue_if_needed
triggers_and_functions.purpose: Optional helper or scheduled job logic to transition scheduled installment occurrences to overdue when due date passes

sql_migration_examples.summary: The following SQL is an implementation template and should be adapted to the existing migration ordering and shared helper functions.
snippets: Create enum types for plan, occurrence, and payment status.
Alter public.expenses to add payment_plan_type, payment_plan_id, payment_plan_occurrence_id.
Create public.payment_plans with shared recurring/installment metadata.
Create public.payment_plan_payments with unique(payment_plan_id, idempotency_key) partial index.

enums: PaymentPlanStatus { active, paused, completed, cancelled, defaulted }
PaymentOccurrenceStatus { scheduled, paid, skipped, cancelled, overdue, partiallyPaid, settledEarly }
PaymentKind { normal, partial, extra, earlyPayoff, correction }

required_models.name: RecurrenceRuleDto
required_models.purpose: Shared schedule DTO for recurring and installment cadence

required_models.name: InstallmentScheduleLineDto
required_models.purpose: Represents authored or generated installment schedule lines

required_models.name: PaymentPlanDto
required_models.purpose: Main plan detail DTO for recurring and installment plans

required_models.name: ScheduledListItemDto
required_models.purpose: Unified list item DTO for recurring page rendering recurring and installment rows together

serialization_guidance: Keep all money fields in cents in transport payloads.
Use immutable DTOs and generated JSON serialization if the codebase already uses freezed/json_serializable patterns.
Expose formatted money/date strings in view models, not transport DTOs.
Use server-provided IDs and status enums as the source of truth for UI state.

design_principles: Use Edge Functions for all mutation-heavy workflows.
Use direct table reads or RPC/views for retrieval where possible.
Perform all multi-table mutations inside a single Postgres transaction.
Every mutation request must include an idempotency key.
Functions must validate auth user, household membership, state transition legality, and monetary reconciliation before writing.

functions.name: create-recurring-plan
functions.purpose: Create a normalized recurring plan while preserving compatibility with existing recurring expense/income flows
validation: end date must not be before anchor date
household membership when householdId is provided
database_operations: optionally dual-write legacy recurring row in public.expenses during migration period

functions.name: create-installment-plan
functions.purpose: Create installment plan and generate explicit occurrence rows upfront
validation: either installmentCount or installmentAmountCents or customSchedule is supplied
if custom schedule mode is true, custom schedule sum must equal total payable
household membership when householdId is provided

functions.name: skip-next-recurring-occurrence
functions.purpose: Skip the next recurring occurrence only
validation: plan belongs to caller or caller has household access
next occurrence exists and is not past end date
database_operations: compute next skippable date from recurrence rule
update payment_plans row and legacy recurring row if dual-write is enabled

functions.name: skip-next-installment-occurrence
functions.purpose: Defer the next pending installment while preserving obligation
validation: find next pending occurrence with status in scheduled, overdue
for MVP, reject defer if occurrence is partially_paid
database_operations: append replacement tail occurrence with same remaining due amount
link skipped occurrence deferred_to_occurrence_id to replacement occurrence

functions.name: mark-plan-occurrence-paid
functions.purpose: Mark an occurrence as fully paid and optionally create/link a booked transaction
validation: occurrence is not cancelled, skipped, or already fully settled
full payment path expects amountCents to cover remaining_amount_cents unless partial mode is explicitly selected
database_operations: create or link booked expense/income row if requested
update occurrence paid_amount_cents, remaining_amount_cents, status, transaction_id

functions.name: mark-plan-occurrence-partially-paid
functions.purpose: Apply partial payment and keep occurrence visible as partially paid
validation: amountCents < remaining_amount_cents for explicit partial mode
occurrence status is scheduled, overdue, or partially_paid
database_operations: set occurrence status to partially_paid unless remaining becomes zero

functions.name: early-payoff-installment-plan
functions.purpose: Settle the remaining balance early while preserving audit trail
validation: for MVP, amountCents must equal exact remaining balance
database_operations: mark unpaid future occurrences as settled_early

functions.name: cancel-payment-plan
functions.purpose: Cancel a recurring or installment plan according to business rules
database_operations: mark future scheduled occurrences as cancelled where applicable
do not delete payments or historical occurrences

retrieval_strategy.reason: Reads should be simple, composable, and RLS-friendly. Mutations should remain in Edge Functions.

algorithm: During migration phase, optionally also create/update the legacy recurring expense row so the existing recurring page continues to function.
Validate principal, interest/fee, total payable, currency, schedule cadence, and household access.
If equal mode, derive installment count or installment amount as needed.
Distribute cents remainder deterministically across earliest installments.
If custom mode, require schedule line sum to equal total payable amount exactly.

equal_installment_distribution.rule: Use floor(total / count) as the base amount and distribute the remainder cents across the earliest installments.

custom_schedule_support.rule: Allow user-authored installment lines only when the sum of due_amount_cents exactly equals total_payable_amount_cents.
notes: Custom mode should still validate increasing dates.
Custom mode should preserve authored amounts exactly.
The system should not auto-round custom lines after validation.

algorithm: Load occurrence and plan with lock/select-for-update semantics.
Set status to paid if remaining becomes zero; else set partially_paid.
Create or link booked transaction in public.expenses when user wants payment reflected in transaction history.

rules: Multiple payment rows may be applied to the same occurrence.
Occurrence remains partially_paid until remaining_amount_cents becomes zero.
For MVP, do not allow skipping/defer on partially_paid occurrences.

calculation_rules: remaining_balance_cents = total_payable_amount_cents - sum(all applied payment amounts except corrections that reverse prior rows)
paid_installments_count = count(occurrences with status in paid, settled_early)
remaining_installments_count = count(occurrences with status in scheduled, overdue, partially_paid)
plan_status becomes completed when remaining_balance_cents reaches zero

high_level_approach.recommendation: Refactor the current add recurring sheet into a plan-aware form with a dedicated Riverpod form controller instead of growing local widget state further.
why: The current sheet already contains substantial local form logic.
Installment-specific fields, preview schedules, validation summary, and household handling will make the current single-widget approach harder to maintain.
A form controller/provider gives cleaner separation for conditional UI and validation.

recommended_flutter_patterns: TextFormField for numeric and free-text fields
DropdownButtonFormField for frequency, interval unit, currency, category
full-width segmented controls for Expense/Income and Recurring/Installment
SwitchListTile or existing themed switch component for secondary toggles such as custom schedule mode, has end date, allow partial payments
Riverpod Notifier or AsyncNotifier for form draft and submit mutation states
shared_fields: privacy scope and owner type when applicable
recurring_form_fields: optional skip-next action available on plan detail
installment_form_fields: equal installments mode or custom schedule mode
installment count or installment amount (one drives the other in equal mode)
custom schedule line editor in advanced mode

best_mvp_ux.financial_input_strategy: Ask user for principal amount and finance fee separately, then derive and display total payable as read-only.
why: Avoids ambiguity between principal and fees.
Scales better to future APR/amortization features.
best_mvp_ux.advanced_mode: custom schedule toggle reveals editable occurrence rows and running total validation

components: summary card with principal, fees, total payable
scrollable list of generated installment lines
running total footer that must equal total payable before submit

installment: occurrence history list with status badges
actions: Mark paid, Record partial payment, Skip/defer, Pay off remaining, Cancel plan

presentation_rules: Skipped installments must remain visible as explicit historical rows.
Replacement occurrences created by defer should appear as future schedule rows.
Recurring skipped occurrences may initially remain implicit unless a materialized occurrence model is added later.

rules: Plan creation must respect household_id, privacy_scope, owner_type, payer_user_id, acknowledged_by, and split-group semantics.
For installment payments, household/shared metadata lives at the plan level and actual cost-sharing continues to be applied on booked payment transactions.
Household viewers should see plan progress only if allowed by privacy rules.
Private plans in a household scope should not leak sensitive details to non-owners.

skip_logic_design.critical_distinction: Recurring skip and installment skip must remain separate at both product and backend logic levels.

recurring_skip.product_meaning: Skip the next occurrence only; future schedule remains unchanged.
recurring_skip.current_implementation: Append next date to recurrence_rule.excluded_dates and update the recurring expense row.
recommended_backend_behavior: Compute next skippable date from recurrence rule.
Persist update to payment_plans.recurrence_rule and legacy expenses.recurrence_rule during migration period.
recurring_skip.auditability: For MVP, excluded_dates is acceptable. Future enhancement can materialize skipped recurring occurrences for better timeline visibility.

installment_skip.product_meaning: Defer the next payment without forgiving the debt.
core_rules: The skipped installment remains visible in history.
The total payable obligation remains unchanged.
The required number of actual settled installments remains unchanged.
A replacement occurrence is appended at the tail.

recommended_implementation.identify_next_pending_occurrence: First occurrence ordered by scheduled_date asc where status in scheduled, overdue and deleted_at is null.
mutations: create replacement tail occurrence with same due_amount_cents
link skipped occurrence deferred_to_occurrence_id to replacement
why_append_tail_not_shift_all_future_row: Avoids rewriting many rows on each defer

installment_skip.multiple_skips: Each skip appends another replacement tail occurrence.
installment_skip.partial_payment_interaction: For MVP, disallow skip/defer once an occurrence has any partial payment applied.
installment_skip.early_payoff_interaction: Skipped and replacement future occurrences may later be marked settled_early when the user pays off the remaining balance.

business_behavior: User can pay remaining balance before all scheduled occurrences have been reached.
Future unpaid occurrences are marked settled_early rather than deleted.
Plan status becomes completed and early_payoff_at is recorded.
early_payoff_design.mvp_rule: Require payoff amount to equal exact remaining balance. Reject overpay for MVP.
backend_steps: Load plan and verify it is active installment plan with remaining_balance_cents > 0.
Mark all future unpaid occurrences as settled_early.
Set plan remaining_balance_cents = 0, plan_status = completed, early_payoff_at = now().
Optionally create a booked expense/income row for the payoff transaction.
flutter_ui: Plan detail CTA named Pay off remaining
confirmation sheet showing exact remaining balance
loading state during Edge Function call
on success, refetch plan detail and occurrence history

recurring: end date, if present, must be after or equal to start date
installment: either installment count or installment amount required in equal mode
custom schedule amounts must sum exactly to total payable
custom schedule dates must be increasing
currency must be present and consistent

edge_function_validation: verify auth user and household membership
verify plan ownership and state transition legality
skip cannot target already paid or cancelled occurrence
early payoff cannot exceed remaining balance in MVP
custom schedule reconciliation must be exact before write

edge_cases: equal installment mode versus custom schedule mode
changing installment amount after plan creation
changing schedule after some installments are paid
early partial payoff as future enhancement
monthly cadence on the 31st and short-month handling
currency conversion and normalized_amount_cents handling on booked transactions
backward compatibility with legacy recurring rows

read_policy_model: Owner user can read their own plans, occurrences, and payments.
Household members can read household-scoped plans only if privacy rules allow.
Soft-deleted rows are excluded from normal select policies.
write_policy_model: Prefer all writes through Edge Functions.
Direct client writes to new plan tables should be disallowed for MVP.
Edge Functions must validate caller ownership and household membership even when running with elevated database access.
privacy_rules: If privacy_scope is private, non-owner household members should not see sensitive installment details.
If privacy_scope is balances_only, household members may see limited summary but not line-item details depending on existing product semantics.
Installment occurrence lists should honor the same visibility rules as the parent plan.
security_notes: Do not trust client-derived remaining balance values.
Use idempotency keys to prevent duplicate payment application.
Preserve audit history; do not hard-delete payment records.

migration_plan.strategy: Dual-read before dual-write. Introduce installment support without breaking existing recurring flows.

phases.name: Foundation
tasks: Add new enum types and tables.
Add new payment_plan_* link columns to public.expenses.

phases.name: Flutter Read Integration
tasks: Render recurring and installment rows in recurring page.
Add status badges and progress summary for installment plans.

phases.name: Flutter Write Integration
tasks: Refactor add recurring sheet into plan-aware form.
Add installment preview and custom schedule editor.
Add plan detail screen actions for skip, partial payment, and early payoff.

phases.name: Recurring Normalization Optional
tasks: Optionally create normalized payment_plans rows for new recurring plans.
Backfill legacy recurring rows into payment_plans when safe.
Keep current recurring expense row compatibility until UI and reads are fully migrated.

backward_compatibility_rules: Existing recurring rows must continue to load and skip correctly.
No existing recurrence_rule consumers should break during migration.
Installments should use only the new normalized tables from day one.

backend: Create enum types and new tables in Supabase migration files.
Add new link columns to public.expenses.
Add indexes, foreign keys, and RLS policies.
Implement create-installment-plan Edge Function with upfront occurrence generation.
Implement skip-next-installment-occurrence Edge Function using defer + append-tail logic.
Implement payment and early payoff Edge Functions.
Create read RPCs or views for plan detail, upcoming occurrences, and unified list feed.
flutter: Add plan-aware repository/service methods for Edge Function calls.
Refactor add_recurring_sheet.dart into shared form sections + installment-specific sections.
Add segmented control for recurring versus installment mode.
Add equal-installment preview and advanced custom schedule editor.
Add installment detail screen with progress summary and occurrence history.
Update recurring_transactions_page.dart to render installment rows and status badges.
testing: Add SQL/Edge Function tests for schedule generation and money reconciliation.
Add Flutter provider tests for form validation and payload generation.
Add widget tests for recurring/installment form toggles and validation messages.
Add integration tests for skip recurring, skip installment, partial payment, and early payoff flows.

acceptance_criteria: User can create a recurring plan without breaking existing recurring behavior.
User can create an installment plan with equal installments.
User can create an installment plan with custom schedule lines whose sum equals total payable.
Skipped recurring occurrence omits only one cycle and future cadence remains unchanged.
Skipped installment occurrence remains visible and creates a replacement tail occurrence.
Partial payment leaves installment occurrence visible as partially paid.
Early payoff marks future unpaid installments as settled early and completes the plan.
Household access and privacy rules are preserved across new tables.
Flutter list/detail screens can clearly distinguish recurring from installment plans.
All monetary invariants are validated server-side.

implement_now: unified list and plan detail reads
defer_until_later: editing installment plan cadence after payments have started
automatic reallocation of overpayments across many future occurrences
technical_debt_to_avoid: Do not store installment financial semantics only in recurrence_rule.
Do not reuse recurring skip logic for installments.
Do not rely only on dynamic occurrence projection for installment plans.
Do not store only aggregate remaining balance without payment audit rows.

future_enhancements: analytics for payoff forecast and delinquency
normalized recurring occurrence materialization for richer timeline history
